calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lindy M. Faris, Esq., 408-292-2100, appearing for Plaintiff Michael Erin Tomren;
Lori Werderitch, Esq., of Greenberg Glusker, 310-553-6310, appearing for Defendant Financial Industry Regulatory;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MICHAEL TOMREN'S Notice Of Hearing

Petitioner Michael Tomren's petition to correct arbitration award is denied. Mr. Tomren has failed to show that any of the limited statutory grounds for correction of an arbitration award under Code of Civil Procedure 1286.6 is applicable. "Generally, courts cannot review arbitration awards for errors of fact or law, even when those errors appear on the face of the award or cause substantial injustice to the parties." (Richey v. AutoNation, Inc. (2015) 60 Cal.4th 909, 916.) "More specifically, courts will not review the validity of the arbitrator's reasoning." (Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11.) The relief sought by Mr. Tomren affects the merits of the arbitrator's award and does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 1286.6(b). Mr. Tomren has failed to show that the arbitrator exceeded his powers and is not entitled to any relief pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 1286.2(a)(4). The arbitrator was not required to give reasons for denying the request for expungement of the records for occurrences 148690 and 1520997. Nor will the Court speculate as to the reasoning and grounds for the arbitrator's decision to deny these requests based on a single remark made by the arbitrator. (See Barnes v. Logan (9th Cir. 1997) 122 F.3d 820, 825 ["[i]t is not for this court to speculate, based on the sparse record and slim arguments, as to the [arbitration] panel's reasoning."].) That is particularly so here, where Petitioner has failed to provide the Court with a transcript or recording of the evidentiary hearing. Furthermore, even an erroneous decision as to the grounds for expungement of the records would not show that the arbitrator exceeded his jurisdiction. (See Safari Associates v. Superior Court (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1403-04 ["California law is clear that 'arbitrators do not "exceed[ ] their powers" . . . merely by rendering an erroneous decision on a legal or factual issue, so long as the issue was within the scope of the controversy submitted to the arbitrators.'"]).

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
